VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW());

parameters.subject: =✅ Approve documents with Gmail and PostgreSQL — run completed at {{ $now.toForm

parameters.content:
### New Candidate Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Role, Stage). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Stamp & Shape Candidate

### Stamp & Shape Candidate

Shapes the item: writes `candidateId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.candidateId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Prepare Approval Packet · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Prepare Approval Packet

Shapes the item: writes `requestId`, `summary`, `requestedBy`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Request Approval via Slack

### Request Approval via Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#hr`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Hold for Decision · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Hold for Decision

Pauses the execution until the resume webhook is called — this is the approval/HITL gate.

Next: Approved?

### Approved?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.body?.decision || $json.decision }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Execute Approved Action (API) · ②→ Record Decline + Notify Requester

### Execute Approved Action (API)

POST https://api.example.com/v1/candidates — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Join Decision Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Record Decline + Notify Requester

Posts a message to Slack — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Join Decision Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Join Decision Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Candidate Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Enrich with Reference Data · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

### Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

Delegates to a reusable sub-workflow (single-responsibility building block) and waits for its result.

Ports: ①→ Record Sub-workflow Result · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Record Sub-workflow Result

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per candidate (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Guard Non-Empty email

### Guard Non-Empty email

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Sort Candidates Newest First

### Sort Candidates Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Candidate Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Gmail · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Gmail

### Escalate Failure to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

# Approve documents with Gmail and PostgreSQL

**Pattern:** Approval Workflow · **Level:** Architect · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Approve documents with Gmail and PostgreSQL. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual time off is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Candidate Form Submission → Stamp & Shape Candidate → Payload Valid? → Prepare Approval Packet → Request Approval via Slack → Hold for Decision → Approved? → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to approval workflow without manual effort
- Anyone automating time off in human resources
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Scales without extra headcount
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Notion integration token; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The workflow pauses on a Wait node configured to resume via webhook — n8n's actual human-in-the-loop primitive, not a polling loop. The resume URL is embedded directly in the approval message so approving is one click, and the paused execution burns no compute for however long the decision takes.

## Practice exercises

About as deep as this pattern goes — extend it:

1. Add a timeout to the Wait node so unanswered approvals auto-decline after N hours.
2. Change the approval channel and confirm the resume link still works.
3. Add a third outcome (e.g. 'needs changes') alongside approve/decline.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🙋 Human in the loop

The workflow pauses at the Wait node until a human posts a decision to the resume URL (included in the approval message). Approve → the action executes; anything else → recorded as declined. No timeout is set by default — add one under the Wait node's options if approvals go stale.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your time off process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
